alignments.preprint_claim_text: A combination of different space transformations is required for meaningful disentanglement of factors of variation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A combination of different space transformations is required for meaningful disentanglement of factors of variation.
alignments.rationale: Identical statement in preprint and publication, preserving meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the proposed model has been demonstrated on real data from tracking detectors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the proposed model is demonstrated on real data from tracking detectors.
alignments.rationale: Same empirical claim with only minor tense wording change; no change to scope or confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel, fully unsupervised approach to track reconstruction is proposed.
alignments.rationale: This explicit high-level framing (novel, fully unsupervised) does not appear among the preprint claims and is newly asserted in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We construct a quantum algorithm to find the globally optimal parse with high constant success probability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We construct a quantum algorithm to find the globally optimal parse with high constant success probability.
alignments.rationale: The construction and claimed success probability are stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our algorithm has runtime R^{n f(R,k)}, where f<1/2 and f approaches 0 exponentially fast with increasing k.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our algorithm has runtime Rnf(R, k), where R is the alphabet size, n the input length, and f < 1/2, with f exponentially fast approaching 0 as k increases.
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies notation and parameter roles (R, n) while preserving the original runtime claim; this is a clarification rather than a semantic change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our algorithm is always more than quadratically faster than its classical counterpart.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our algorithm is always more than quadratically faster than its classical counterpart.
alignments.rationale: The performance-comparison conclusion is carried over verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We modified our procedure to recover a finite beam width variant, enabling a stronger empirical speedup while retaining higher accuracy than possible classically.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We modified our procedure to recover a finite beam width variant, enabling an even stronger empirical speedup while retaining higher accuracy than possible classically.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same contribution but slightly strengthens phrasing ('even stronger'); overall the claim is the same contribution with minor wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The quantum beam search decoder applied to Mozilla's implementation of Baidu's DeepSpeech neural net exhibits a power law word rank frequency.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We apply this quantum beam search decoder to Mozilla’s implementation of Baidu’s DeepSpeech neural net, which exhibits a power law word rank frequency.
alignments.rationale: The empirical application is the same, but the publication marks this result with lower certainty (medium), so confidence is tempered while the semantic content is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using $L_2$ regularization for fields and group $L_1$ for couplings is very effective for sparse couplings compared to $L_2$ and $L_1$.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using L2 regularization for fields and group L1 for couplings is very effective for sparse couplings compared to L2 and L1.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same empirical result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two regularization parameters can be tuned to yield equal values for both the sample and ensemble averages of evolutionary energy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two regularization parameters can be tuned to yield equal values for both the sample and ensemble averages of evolutionary energy.
alignments.rationale: The statement is the same but the publication lists this with lower certainty (medium vs high), so the confidence is reduced.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Adam method can be modified to make stepsize proportional to the gradient for sparse couplings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Adam method can be modified to make stepsize proportional to the gradient for sparse couplings and to use a soft-thresholding function for group L1.
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the algorithmic contribution by adding the soft-thresholding detail; this clarifies and strengthens the contribution and is presented with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recovering the pairwise correlations in the resolution of a total energy is harder for natural proteins than for protein-like sequences.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recovering the pairwise correlations in the resolution of a total energy is harder for natural proteins than for protein-like sequences.
alignments.rationale: This empirical comparison appears verbatim and with the same reported certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Selective temperature for folding/structural constraints in protein evolution is estimated.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Selective temperature for folding/structural constraints in protein evolution is estimated.
alignments.rationale: The estimation claim is preserved with similar (low) certainty in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed neural model captures language diffusion tendencies in author communities through time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed neural model captures language diffusion tendencies in author communities through time.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is stated identically with no change in meaning or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Conditioning language models with author and temporal vector states allows for leveraging latent dependencies between text contexts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Conditioning language models with author and temporal vector states allows for leveraging latent dependencies between text contexts.
alignments.rationale: Same methodological/result claim in both versions without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model outperforms several temporal and non-temporal language baselines on two real-world corpora.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model outperforms several temporal and non-temporal language baselines on two real-world corpora.
alignments.rationale: Performance claim preserved exactly between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model learns meaningful author representations that vary through time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model learns meaningful author representations that vary through time.
alignments.rationale: The representational claim remains the same with similar reported certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LazyBum achieves comparable accuracy with a lower execution time compared to the original OneBM and two other propositionalization methods (nFOIL and MODL) on most datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LazyBum achieves comparable accuracy with a lower execution time compared to the original OneBM and two other propositionalization methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication drops the qualifier 'on most datasets' making the claim more general; this is a minor wording/clarification that broadens scope slightly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The transformation from relational to propositional format is generally not lossless, as different relational structures may be mapped onto the same feature vector.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The transformation from relational to propositional format is generally not lossless, as different relational structures may be mapped onto the same feature vector.
alignments.rationale: The conceptual conclusion is the same but presented with reduced asserted certainty in the publication (medium vs high).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LazyBum interleaves OneBM's feature construction method with a decision tree learner to guide the propositionalization process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LazyBum interleaves OneBM’s feature construction method with a decision tree learner to guide the propositionalization process.
alignments.rationale: The methodological contribution is the same but is stated with higher certainty in the publication (publication marks it high confidence), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.pub_claim_text: It is hard to define a feature space that contains all and only those features that are needed for the learning task.
alignments.rationale: This general limitation/observation is not present among the preprint claims and appears newly in the publication as an added statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nonconvex penalties that satisfy certain regularity conditions are guaranteed to perform well with standard optimization algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state a general guarantee that nonconvex penalties will perform well with standard optimizers; it instead introduces specific penalties and places them in frameworks, so the general theoretical guarantee is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper examines new and currently existing nonconvex penalties for DNN regularization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper introduces two new penalty functions, Laplace and arctan, for regularization in machine learning models.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's general claim about examining penalties is refined in the publication to specifically introducing two new penalties (Laplace and arctan), narrowing the original broader statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of all penalties is assessed with DNN analyses of seven datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the Laplace and arctan penalty functions is compared empirically with existing penalty functions on seven different datasets.
alignments.rationale: Both describe empirical comparison on seven datasets, but the publication specifies the comparison focuses on the new Laplace and arctan penalties, narrowing the preprint's broader wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Theoretical justifications for the new penalties are provided in the paper.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new penalty functions fit into recently introduced statistical and optimization frameworks.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly situates the new penalties within statistical/optimization frameworks, which constitutes a clarification of the preprint's claim of providing theoretical justification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep neural networks with rectified power units (RePU) provide better approximations for smooth functions compared to traditional ReLU networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep neural networks with rectified power units (RePU) provide better approximations for smooth functions compared to traditional ReLU networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim about RePU networks offering improved approximation for smooth functions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithms can construct PowerNets that represent polynomials with no approximation error.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PowerNets can represent polynomials with no approximation error.
alignments.rationale: The publication echoes the preprint's result that PowerNets can exactly represent polynomials.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The error of the best polynomial approximation serves as an upper bound for the best RePU network approximation error for general smooth functions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The error of the best polynomial approximation serves as an upper bound for the best RePU network approximation error.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same theoretical relationship between polynomial approximation error and RePU network approximation error.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A PowerNet with n layers can exactly represent polynomials up to degree s^n, where s is the power of RePUs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PowerNets with $n$ hidden layers can exactly represent polynomials up to degree $s^n$, where $s$ is the power of RePUs.
alignments.rationale: Both versions state the same exact representational capacity result for PowerNets.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed PowerNets have potential applications in scenarios where high accuracy and smoothness are required.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed PowerNets have potential applications in scenarios where high accuracy and smoothness are required.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the preprint's stated potential applications for PowerNets.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduced generative model is capable of tailoring anticancer compounds for a specific biomolecular profile.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or repeat claims about a generative model tailored to biomolecular profiles; instead it presents high-level drug-development statistics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The molecule generation can be biased towards compounds with high predicted inhibitory effect against individual cell lines or specific cancer sites.
alignments.rationale: No corresponding empirical claim about biasing molecule generation toward specific inhibitory effects appears in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach verifies candidate drugs generated against specific cancer types and finds the highest structural similarity to existing compounds with known efficacy.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report verification of generated candidates or similarity findings; it instead contains unrelated industry-level statistics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method is expected to transform in silico anticancer drug design by leveraging the biomolecular characteristics of the disease.
alignments.rationale: The publication omits transformative claims about the proposed method and instead reports general facts about drug development timelines and costs.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Less than 0.01% of drug candidates obtain market approval.
alignments.rationale: This publication-level statistic about approval rates does not appear in the preprint and is therefore an added claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The estimated time until market release for drug candidates is 10–15 years.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces an industry time-to-market estimate not present in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The costs for developing a drug range between one to three billion dollars.
alignments.rationale: The publication's cost estimate for drug development is new relative to the preprint's scientific claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An automatic method based on deep learning can analyze Achilles tendon condition and estimate its healing progress on ultrasound images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An automatic method based on deep learning can analyze Achilles tendon condition and estimate its healing progress on ultrasound images.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint's contribution about a DL-based automatic method for Achilles tendon assessment on ultrasound.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models developed demonstrate a high correlation with the assessment of expert radiologists regarding key parameters of healing progress.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models developed show high correlation with the assessment of expert radiologists regarding key parameters of healing progress.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication report the same result of high correlation between model outputs and expert radiologist assessments.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Parameters associated with intratendinous healing processes are better modeled with sagittal ultrasound slices.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Parameters associated with intratendinous healing processes are better modeled with sagittal ultrasound slices.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint's specific finding about sagittal slices modeling intratendinous healing parameters.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Ultrasound imaging is quantitatively useful for clinical assessment of the Achilles tendon healing process and should complement magnetic resonance imaging.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Ultrasound imaging is quantitatively useful for clinical assessment of the Achilles tendon healing process.
alignments.rationale: The publication separates the preprint's combined claim into distinct claims; the matched publication claim preserves the core quantitative-usefulness assertion while the complementary-to-MRI point appears as a separate publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep Networks are susceptible to various types of noise, including adversarial attacks and corrupted inputs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep Networks are susceptible to various types of noise, including adversarial attacks and corrupted inputs.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the preprint's conclusion about susceptibility of deep networks to noise and attacks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A formal definition of robustness is introduced, which can be viewed as a localized Lipschitz constant of the network function.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A formal definition of robustness is introduced, which can be viewed as a localized Lipschitz constant of the network function.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes the same contribution of a formal robustness definition.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new notion of robustness is compared to existing ones and its connections with methods in the literature are studied.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new notion of robustness is compared to existing ones and its connections with methods in the literature are studied.
alignments.rationale: Both versions state the same comparative and contextual analysis of the proposed robustness notion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The robustness metric is evaluated through experiments on various competitive vision datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The robustness metric is evaluated through experiments on various competitive vision datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same experimental evaluation of the robustness metric on vision datasets as the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Resolution-wise Attention Module (RAM) learns a group of weights to represent the different importance of feature maps across resolutions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Resolution-wise Attention Module (RAM) learns weights that represent the importance of feature maps across different resolutions.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with minor wording changes; the meaning and scope are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gradual Pyramid Refinement (GPR) gradually merges every two feature maps from low to high resolutions to regress final human keypoint heatmaps.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gradual Pyramid Refinement (GPR) merges feature maps from low to high resolutions to improve the regression of human keypoint heatmaps.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same mechanism but removes the specific 'every two' phrasing and frames it as improving regression, a small clarification rather than a substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The enhanced resolution-wise features learnt by CNN lead to more accurate human keypoint locations.
alignments.rationale: No explicit publication claim restates this causal statement about resolution-wise features improving localization, so the preprint claim is not present in the final claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methods achieve state-of-the-art performance with an average precision of 77.7 on the COCO val2017 set and 77.0 on the test-dev2017 set.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methods proposed in this paper achieve state-of-the-art performance on the MS-COCO dataset with an average precision of 77.7 on the COCO va12017 set.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same 77.7 val2017 result and retains the SOTA claim; minor typographic differences do not alter the claim's meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results were obtained without using an extra human keypoint training dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methods achieve an average precision of 77.0 on the test-dev2017 set without using any extra human keypoint training dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the limitation/setting that no extra keypoint dataset was used, embedding it with the test-dev performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Distributed Equivalent Substitution (DES) training introduces fully synchronous training to large-scale recommendation systems for the first time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Distributed Equivalent Substitution (DES) training introduces fully synchronous training to large-scale recommendation systems for the first time.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution verbatim, with no meaningful change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DES training reduces communication, making the training of commercial recommender systems converge faster and reach better CTR.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DES training reduces communication, making the training of commercial recommender systems converge faster and reach better CTR.
alignments.rationale: The result claim is carried over exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Due to the use of synchronous training, DES achieves higher AUC (Area Under ROC) on large-scale Deep Learning Recommendation Models (DLRMs).
alignments.pub_claim_text: Due to the use of synchronous training, DES achieves higher AUC (Area Under ROC) on large-scale Deep Learning Recommendation Models (DLRMs).
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this performance claim with identical framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments show that DES training outperforms the state-of-the-art PS-based training framework, achieving up to 68.7% communication savings and higher throughput.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments show that DES training outperforms the state-of-the-art PS-based training framework, achieving up to 68.7% communication savings and higher throughput.
alignments.rationale: The experimental result and quantitative savings are unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed cascaded system combines both 2D and 3D convolutional neural networks to effectively segment hepatic lesions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed cascaded system effectively segments hepatic lesions by combining both 2D and 3D convolutional neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same combined 2D/3D cascaded approach and its effective segmentation of hepatic lesions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The 2D network segments the liver and larger tumors, while the 3D network detects small lesions that are often missed in a 2D segmentation design.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The 2D network segments liver and large liver lesions, while the 3D network detects small lesions that are often missed in 2D segmentation.
alignments.rationale: Both versions describe the complementary roles of 2D and 3D networks with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm achieves a Dice score per case of 68.1%, which is the best among all non pre-trained models and the second best among published methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm achieves a Dice score per subject of 68.1%, which is the best among all non-pre-trained models and the second-best among published methods.
alignments.rationale: The numeric result and comparative ranking are preserved, with only minor wording normalization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two-fold cross-validation reveals over- and under-segmentation issues in the LiTS annotations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two-fold cross-validation reveals over- and under-segmentation issues in the annotations of the LiTS dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this observation about annotation issues, phrased slightly differently.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: InceptionTime is on par with HIVE-COTE in terms of accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: InceptionTime is on par with HIVE-COTE in terms of accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The accuracy comparison is carried over identically into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: InceptionTime can learn from 1,500 time series in one hour.
alignments.pub_claim_text: InceptionTime can learn from 1500 time series in one hour.
alignments.rationale: The performance/time claim is equivalent, differing only in numeric formatting.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: InceptionTime can learn from 8M time series in 13 hours.
alignments.pub_claim_text: InceptionTime can learn from 8M time series in 13 hours.
alignments.rationale: The large-scale training time claim is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HIVE-COTE cannot be applied to many real-world datasets due to its high training time complexity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: HIVE-COTE cannot be applied to many real-world datasets due to its high training time complexity.
alignments.rationale: The limitation regarding HIVE-COTE's practicality due to time complexity is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BERT's hidden states contain valuable information that is crucial for understanding its internal functioning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: BERT's hidden states contain equally valuable information as attention weights for understanding its internal functioning.
alignments.rationale: The publication modifies the claim by adding an explicit comparison to attention weights (stating equivalence), which changes the claim's content beyond a simple rewording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The transformations within BERT go through phases that are related to traditional pipeline tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The transformations within BERT go through phases that are related to traditional pipeline tasks.
alignments.rationale: This observation about transformation phases is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fine-tuning has little impact on the models' semantic abilities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fine-tuning has little impact on the models' semantic abilities.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion about fine-tuning's limited impact on semantic abilities.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Prediction errors can be recognized in the vector representations of even early layers of the model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Prediction errors can be recognized in the vector representations of even early layers of the model.
alignments.rationale: The claim about early-layer vector representations containing error signals is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed meta-neural-network demonstrates a compact and high-resolution capability for real-time recognition of complicated objects through acoustic scattering analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A purely-passive and small-footprint meta-neural-network can recognize complicated objects in real-time by analyzing acoustic scattering. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core result but adds the detail that the device is purely passive; this refines/clarifies the original claim without changing its scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The meta-neural-network mimics standard neural networks despite its small footprint due to the unique capabilities of its metamaterial unit cells, or 'meta-neurons'. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The meta-neural-network mimics a standard neural network due to the unique capability of its metamaterial unit-cells to produce deep-subwavelength phase shifts as training parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication gives a more specific mechanism (deep-subwavelength phase shifts as training parameters) while preserving the original claim that the meta-neurons allow the device to mimic neural networks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The device exhibits intelligence to perform tasks that address the trade-off between size, cost, energy consumption, and recognition speed and accuracy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The device demonstrates the ability to perform tasks such as handwritten digit recognition and discerning misaligned orbital-angular-momentum vortices. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces the broad trade-off/intelligence framing with concrete task demonstrations (specific recognition tasks), narrowing the scope to exemplars and presenting them with higher asserted confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mechanism opens new routes to metamaterial-based deep-learning paradigms and enables the development of conceptual devices like smart transducers for automatic signal analysis. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed mechanism opens the route to new metamaterial-based deep-learning paradigms. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core contribution claim about opening routes to metamaterial-based deep-learning; it omits the illustrative mention of smart transducers but does not change the main contribution statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed weakly supervised learning-based approach can effectively learn to localize the discriminative evidence for a diagnostic label from weakly labeled training data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed weakly supervised learning-based approach can effectively learn to localize the discriminative evidence for a diagnostic label from weakly labeled training data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results show that the proposed method can reliably pinpoint the location of cancerous evidence supporting the decision of interest. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results show that the proposed method can reliably pinpoint the location of cancerous evidence supporting the decision of interest. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result claim is carried forward unchanged in wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method achieves competitive performance on glimpse-level and slide-level histopathologic cancer detection tasks. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method achieves competitive performance on glimpse-level and slide-level histopathologic cancer detection tasks. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same performance claim with identical scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed classification-specific part estimation uses initial predictions and back-propagation of feature importance to estimate relevant image regions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed classification-specific part estimation method uses initial predictions and back-propagation of feature importance to estimate relevant image regions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The detected parts are selected based on a-posteriori classification knowledge and have an intrinsic spatial extent that is determined automatically. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The detected parts in the proposed method are selected based on a-posteriori classification knowledge and have an intrinsic spatial extent that is determined automatically. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result claim is carried forward verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The part selection method demonstrates effectiveness when used in conjunction with the extracted part features on various widely-used fine-grained datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The part selection method demonstrates effectiveness when used in conjunction with the extracted part features on various widely-used fine-grained datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the experimental effectiveness claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Multi-Weight Traffic Graph Convolutional (MW-TGC) network outperformed comparative models in traffic forecasting at two urban sites. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Multi-Weight Traffic Graph Convolutional (MW-TGC) network outperformed comparative models in traffic forecasting at two urban sites. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same performance claim with identical scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MW-TGC network reduced variance in the heterogeneous urban-mix network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The MW-TGC network reduced variance in the heterogeneous urban-mix network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The variance-reduction result is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MW-TGC network can provide robust traffic forecasting performance across variations in spatial complexity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The MW-TGC network can provide robust traffic forecasting performance across variations in spatial complexity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim about robustness is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framelet representation of the tensor nuclear norm improves tensor completion for third-order tensors. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framelet representation of the tensor nuclear norm improves third-order tensor recovery. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same core contribution (improved recovery/completion for third-order tensors) with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The minimization model developed in this paper is convex and allows for the identification of global minimizers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The minimization model developed in this paper is convex and allows for the attainment of global minimizers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the convexity and global minimizer property with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms other testing methods on various types of multi-dimensional data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms other testing methods on various types of multi-dimensional data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The empirical performance claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The representation of each tube using framelet basis leads to a sparse representation, which is beneficial when the matrix slices are highly correlated. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The representation of each tube using framelet basis leads to a sparse representation, which is beneficial when the matrix slices of the original tensor are highly correlated. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion about sparsity benefits when matrix slices are highly correlated, with only minor wording adjustments.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning models are not transparent and behave as a black box, raising safety issues in Cyber-Physical Systems. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning models are not transparent and behave as a black box, raising safety issues in Cyber-Physical Systems. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same limitation verbatim, with no substantive change in meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ML models work well in the training space but cannot extrapolate beyond it, leading to higher error rates in feature spaces lacking training data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ML models work well in the training space but cannot extrapolate beyond it, leading to higher error rates in underrepresented feature spaces. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining the different parts of the λ-Architecture is necessary to improve the overall accuracy and performance of the platform. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper provides a concrete framework for formal verification of reachability properties on imperative data-aware business processes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results show statistically significant improvements in grasp success rate when using simulated images over real camera images, particularly with angled real camera viewpoints. (certainty=high, type=result)
